info.title: npm Registry API — SDK Extensions
info.description:
Vendor extensions to the official npm registry API surface that are implemented
by the `safe-npm-sdk` on top of standard primitives (GET/PUT/DELETE against the
packument document and its tarball attachments).

These endpoints are NOT part of the published `openapi.json`. They are documented
here at the same specification standard so that consumers can discover the exact
HTTP traffic the SDK's high-level operations produce. Each operation is tagged
with the SDK function that drives it and notes which step of that function it
represents.

Where a path reuses an existing component from `openapi.json` (a parameter,
response, or security scheme), it references it by `$ref` for consistency.

**Conventions specific to these extensions:**

- CouchDB optimistic concurrency is expressed via a `/-rev/{rev}` path segment
  on write operations. The `{rev}` is the packument's current `_rev`, captured
  by a preceding `GET` and threaded through every subsequent mutation.
- All write steps require 2FA. The one-time password is sent in the `npm-otp`
  header; `npm-auth-type: web` and `npm-command` enable the WebAuthn flow.
  A missing or invalid OTP yields `401`/`403` (see `responses-Unauthorized` /
  `Forbidden`).

tags.name: Unpublish
tags.description:
Low-level HTTP operations driven by `unpublishPackage()`. Removing a live
  published version is a multi-step protocol against the CouchDB-revisioned
  packument document:

  1. `GET /{escapedPackageName}` — read the packument and its `_rev`.
  2. `PUT /{escapedPackageName}/-rev/{rev}` — write the packument back with the
     target version dropped from `versions`/`time` and `dist-tags.latest`
     reassigned to the greatest remaining version.
  3. `GET /{escapedPackageName}` — read the fresh `_rev` produced by step 2.
  4. `DELETE /{escapedPackageName}/-/{tarball}/-rev/{rev}` — delete the tarball
     binary attachment for the removed version.

  When the removed version was the package's only version, step 4 becomes a full
  `DELETE /{escapedPackageName}/-rev/{rev}` that removes the entire package.

  The operations below are the individual steps; the SDK orchestrates them in
  the order shown. None is meant to be called in isolation unless you are
  reimplementing the protocol.

tags.name: Profile
tags.description:
User profile endpoints backed by `getProfile()` / `updateProfile()` / `changePassword()` / `enableTwoFactor()` / `disableTwoFactor()`, plus `lookupAvatar()` — a best-effort avatar resolver that chains several GETs.

The two official `/−/npm/v1/user` routes come from the registry profile docs; write steps require 2FA (`npm-otp`).

`lookupAvatar()` resolves a username's avatar by trying, in order: the authenticated profile's email → a verified Gravatar URL; the registry user documents `GET /−/user/{username}` and `GET /−/user/org.couchdb.user:{username}` (reading an `avatar` field); and a registry maintainer search (`maintainer:{username}`) whose matching publisher/maintainer email is verified against Gravatar. The first hit wins and is tagged with its source (`authenticated-profile` / `registry-profile` / `maintainer-gravatar` / `none`). Avatar resolution never throws — a total miss yields `{ avatarUrl: null, source: "none" }`. The Gravatar verification (`HEAD https://gravatar.com/avatar/{sha256}?s=128&d=404`) is an external HTTP call outside the registry; it uses SHA-256 of the email via Web Crypto and returns `null` on any non-2xx or failed response.

tags.name: Login
tags.description: Authentication endpoints backed by `loginCouch()` and `loginWeb()`, ported from `npm-profile`. Couch login is a CouchDB-revisioned `PUT` against `/−/user/org.couchdb.user:{username}` (with a `409 → GET ?write=true → −rev re-PUT` flow using HTTP Basic auth). Web login posts `/−/v1/login` to obtain a `(loginUrl, doneUrl)` pair, then polls the `doneUrl` (200 → token, 202 → retry, other → error). The SDK never opens a browser.

get.description:
**Used internally by `unpublishPackage()`** as steps 1 and 3. Fetches the
current packument document to obtain its CouchDB `_rev` (step 1) and again
after the `PUT` to obtain the freshly incremented `_rev` (step 3, needed to
authorize the tarball deletion).

This is a standard packument read and is identical to the registry's public
package document endpoint; it is restated here only to document its role in
the unpublish protocol. No 2FA is required to read.

delete.description:
**Fallback branch inside `unpublishPackage()`.** When the package's only
remaining version has just been removed and the post-`PUT` read could not be
obtained (so no fresh `_rev` is known), the SDK issues a bare `DELETE
/{escapedPackageName}` rather than `DELETE /{escapedPackageName}/-rev/{rev}`.
The registry rejects this without a current revision; the operation exists in
the spec only to document the SDK's defensive behavior.

Prefer the `-rev/{rev}` form (`unpublish-deletePackage`).

put.summary: Rewrite packument without the version (unpublish step 2)
put.description:
**Used internally by `unpublishPackage()`** as step 2. Writes the packument
back with the target version removed from `versions` and `time`, and with
`dist-tags.latest` reassigned to the greatest remaining version when it
pointed at the removed version. The `{rev}` MUST be the `_rev` captured in
step 1; a stale revision yields `409 Conflict`.

The request body is the full modified packument (passthrough of the document
read in step 1 with the described mutations). 2FA is mandatory.

requestBody.description:
The modified packument. The SDK passes through every field it read in step
1 and only adjusts `versions`, `time`, and (when needed) `dist-tags.latest`.
The shape shown is a representative subset.

versions.description: All versions except the one being unpublished.

time.description: Version timestamps with the unpublished version's entry removed.

dist-tags.description:
Dist tags; `latest` is reassigned to the greatest remaining version when
the removed version previously held it.

delete.description:
**Used internally by `unpublishPackage()`** as step 4 when the removed
version was the package's only version. Deletes the entire packument
document. The `{rev}` MUST be the fresh `_rev` obtained from the post-`PUT`
`GET`; a stale revision yields `409 Conflict`. 2FA is mandatory.

delete.summary: Delete the version's tarball attachment (unpublish step 4)
delete.description:
**Used internally by `unpublishPackage()`** as step 4 (non-only-version
branch). Deletes the tarball binary attachment of the removed version. The
attachment name `{tarball}` is derived from the removed version's
`dist.tarball` URL (e.g. `npm-2.0.0.tgz`). The `{rev}` MUST be the fresh
`_rev` obtained from the post-`PUT` `GET`; a stale revision yields
`409 Conflict`. 2FA is mandatory.

The SDK skips this step entirely if no tarball path could be derived from the
packument.

get.description: `getProfile()`. Reads the authenticated user's profile document. No 2FA required.

post.description: `updateProfile()` / `changePassword()` / `enableTwoFactor()` / `disableTwoFactor()`. Posts a profile update request. Empty-string values are coerced to `null`. Enabling 2FA is a two-step flow: POST `{tfa:{mode,password}}` returns an `otpauth://` URL, then POST `{tfa:[code]}` returns recovery codes. Requires 2FA (`npm-otp`).

get.summary: Read a registry user document by username (lookupAvatar registry-profile link)
get.description:
**Used internally by `lookupAvatar()`** as the first registry user-document
probe (the bare-username id shape). Reads an `avatar`/`avatarUrl`/
`avatar_url` field; an absent or blank field falls through to the
`org.couchdb.user:`-prefixed shape, then to the maintainer-search link.
Anonymous — npm no longer reliably exposes an avatar here, so this step
frequently misses and the resolver continues down the fallback chain.

No 2FA is required to read.

200.description: A permissive JSON object; the SDK only reads an avatar field from it.

avatar.description: Legacy npm avatar path (e.g. `/avatar/{md5}`) or absolute URL.

get.summary: Read existing user document (conflict branch, ?write=true / lookupAvatar link)
get.description:
Two roles:

- `loginCouch()` step 2a, fired after a `409` conflict. `GET …?write=true` reads the existing user document to capture its CouchDB `_rev` for the subsequent `−rev` re-PUT. Anonymous.
- `lookupAvatar()` registry-profile link (second id shape tried). Reads an `avatar`/`avatarUrl`/`avatar_url` field; an absent or blank field falls through to the next fallback. Anonymous.

parameters.name: write
parameters.description: Set to `true`; the registry returns a fresh `_rev` for a conditional write.

put.description: `loginCouch()` step 1. `PUT` a fresh user document `{_id, name, password, type:'user', roles:[], date}` **anonymously** (login is how a token is obtained). On success returns the user document with a `token`. On `409` (user exists) the SDK falls through to GET + re-PUT. On `400` the user does not exist.

put.summary: Re-PUT the user document at the current rev (conflict branch)
put.description: `loginCouch()` step 2b, fired after reading the existing document on a `409`. Re-PUTs the merged user document at `/-rev/{rev}` using HTTP Basic auth (`username:password`) so the registry accepts the update. The `{rev}` is the `_rev` from the preceding GET. OTP is sent when provided.

post.description: `loginWeb()`. Posts an empty body to obtain a `(loginUrl, doneUrl)` pair. The caller opens `loginUrl` in a browser (the SDK never does); the SDK then polls `doneUrl` (see the `done()` handle). When the configured registry is a proxy/mirror, the canonical `registry.npmjs.org` host in `doneUrl` is rewritten to the registry origin. Anonymous.

get.description: `loginWeb().done()`. Polls the `doneUrl` returned by `/−/v1/login`. `200` with a `token` completes the flow; `202` means pending (honor `Retry-After` or the configured interval, then poll again); any other status is an error. NOTE: `doneUrl` is an absolute per-response URL (the canonical host may be rewritten to the configured registry); this path documents the standard `/−/v1/done?authId=…` shape. The SDK times out after `timeoutMs` or when the caller's `signal` aborts.

parameters.name: authId
parameters.description: Auth session id correlating the login with the done poll.

202.description: Still pending. Honor the `Retry-After` response header (seconds) or the configured poll interval, then poll again.

retry-after.description: Seconds to wait before the next poll.

Packument.description:
The package's packument document, as read by `unpublishPackage()` step 1/3.
The shape is a permissive subset; the registry returns additional fields that
the SDK passes through verbatim on the subsequent PUT.

CouchUserDocument.description: A CouchDB user document. On successful login it carries a `token`.

CouchUserNotFound.description: Bad Request — no user exists with the given username.

WebLoginInit.description: The `(loginUrl, doneUrl)` pair that starts a web login flow.

loginUrl.description: URL the user visits in a browser.

doneUrl.description: URL the SDK polls for completion.

WebLoginToken.description: Web login completed; the session token.

Packument.description:
A CouchDB-revisioned packument document. The SDK reads it for `_rev`, mutates
`versions`/`time`/`dist-tags`, and writes it back. All other fields are
passed through unchanged.

_rev.description: CouchDB revision token. Required to authorize mutations via `/-rev/{rev}`.

versions.description:
Published versions, keyed by semver. Each entry is that version's
package.json-shaped manifest (see `PackumentVersion`).

time.description: ISO-8601 timestamps keyed by version, plus `created`/`modified` meta keys.

PackumentVersion.description:
A single version entry within a packument. The SDK reads `dist.tarball` from
it to derive the tarball attachment path for deletion.

tarball.description:
Absolute URL of this version's tarball, e.g.
                https://registry.npmjs.org/demo-pkg/-/demo-pkg-1.0.0.tgz

Tfa.description: Polymorphic 2FA state. `null`/`false` = disabled; an `otpauth://` string = setup in progress (render as QR); a `string[]` = one-time recovery codes; `{mode,pending}` = setup pending.

Profile.description: A user's profile, as returned by GET /-/npm/v1/user.

ProfileUpdateRequest.description: A profile update request for POST /-/npm/v1/user. All fields optional; empty strings are coerced to `null`.

CouchUserDocument.description: A CouchDB user document used by the couch login flow.

_rev.description: CouchDB revision (present on read / conflict branch).

token.description: Session token returned on successful login.

PackumentRev.name: rev

schema.description:
A CouchDB revision token, e.g. `1-aaaa` or `2-bbbb`. Captured from a prior
`GET /{escapedPackageName}` and threaded through every write step to express
optimistic concurrency.

PackumentRev.description: CouchDB document revision used by the `/-rev/{rev}` path segment.

TarballName.name: tarball
TarballName.description:
Tarball attachment filename (the basename of the version's `dist.tarball`),
e.g. `demo-pkg-1.0.0.tgz`.

NpmOtp.name: npm-otp
NpmOtp.description:
One-time password for 2FA. Mandatory on every write step of unpublish.
Omitting it yields `401`/`403` with a challenge — see
`responses-Unauthorized` / `Forbidden`.

NpmAuthType.name: npm-auth-type
NpmAuthType.description: Set to `web` to engage the WebAuthn flow. Paired with `npm-command`.

NpmCommand.name: npm-command
NpmCommand.description:
CLI command context forwarded to the registry (e.g. `token`). Used
alongside `npm-auth-type: web` for the WebAuthn flow.

Username.name: username
Username.description: An npm username. URL-encoded into the CouchDB user document id `org.couchdb.user:{username}`.
